en: Now Joab the son of Zeruiah knew that the king's heart went out to Absalom.
zh: 洗魯雅的兒子約押知道王的心想念押沙龍，

en: And Joab sent to Tekoa and brought from there a wise woman and said to her, "Pretend to be a mourner and put on mourning garments. Do not anoint yourself with oil, but behave like a woman who has been mourning many days for the dead.
zh: 就派人到提哥亞去，從那裡帶了一位聰明的婦人來，對她說：“你要假裝居喪的，穿上喪服，不要用油膏抹身體。要裝成一個為死者居喪很久的婦人。

en: Go to the king and speak thus to him." So Joab put the words in her mouth.
zh: 然後進去見王，對他這樣這樣說。”於是約押把要說的話教了她。

en: When the woman of Tekoa came to the king, she fell on her face to the ground and paid homage and said, "Save me, O king."
zh: 提哥亞婦人進去見王，俯伏在地，叩拜他，說：“王啊！求你幫助我。”

en: And the king said to her, "What is your trouble?" She answered, "Alas, I am a widow; my husband is dead.
zh: 王問她：“你有甚麼事呢？”她回答：“我實在是個寡婦，我的丈夫死了。

en: And your servant had two sons, and they quarreled with one another in the field. There was no one to separate them, and one struck the other and killed him.
zh: 婢女有兩個兒子；有一天，他們兩人在田裡打架，當時沒有人勸開他們，這個就擊打那個，竟把他打死了。

en: And now the whole clan has risen against your servant, and they say, 'Give up the man who struck his brother, that we may put him to death for the life of his brother whom he killed.' And so they would destroy the heir also. Thus they would quench my coal that is left and leave to my husband neither name nor remnant on the face of the earth."
zh: 看哪！現在全家族的人都起來攻擊婢女，說：‘你要把那擊殺他兄弟的兇手交出來，好讓我們把他處死，以償他兄弟的命，也可以滅絕那繼承產業的。’這樣他們要使我所剩下的炭火也都熄滅了，不讓我丈夫在這世上留下名字，或留下後代。”

en: Then the king said to the woman, "Go to your house, and I will give orders concerning you."
zh: 王對那婦人說：“你回家去吧！我必為你下令辦這件事。”

en: And the woman of Tekoa said to the king, "On me be the guilt, my lord the king, and on my father's house; let the king and his throne be guiltless."
zh: 提哥亞婦人又對王說：“我主我王，願這罪歸於我和我父的家；願王和王的王位與這罪無關。”

en: The king said, "If anyone says anything to you, bring him to me, and he shall never touch you again."
zh: 王說：“對你說這事的，你就把他帶到我這裡來，他必不會再打擾你了。”

en: Then she said, "Please let the king invoke the LORD your God, that the avenger of blood kill no more, and my son be not destroyed." He said, "As the LORD lives, not one hair of your son shall fall to the ground."
zh: 婦人說：“願王記念耶和華你的 神，不許報血仇的人多行殺戮，免得他們滅絕我的兒子。”王說：“我指著永活的耶和華起誓，你的兒子連一根頭髮也不會落在地上。”

en: Then the woman said, "Please let your servant speak a word to my lord the king." He said, "Speak."
zh: 那婦人又說：“請允許婢女對我主我王再說一句話。”王說：“你說吧。”

en: And the woman said, "Why then have you planned such a thing against the people of God? For in giving this decision the king convicts himself, inasmuch as the king does not bring his banished one home again.
zh: 婦人說：“你為甚麼想出這樣的事來陷害 神的子民呢？王說了這話，如果不讓自己逃亡的人回來，就是個有罪的人了。

en: We must all die; we are like water spilled on the ground, which cannot be gathered up again. But God will not take away life, and he devises means so that the banished one will not remain an outcast.
zh: 我們都是必死的，像水潑在地上不能收回來一樣；但 神並不取去人的命，反而設法使逃亡的人不致永遠離開他逃亡。

en: Now I have come to say this to my lord the king because the people have made me afraid, and your servant thought, 'I will speak to the king; it may be that the king will perform the request of his servant.
zh: 現在我來對我主我王說這話，是因為眾民使我害怕，所以婢女想：‘現在我要對王說，也許王會成全婢女所求的。’

en: For the king will hear and deliver his servant from the hand of the man who would destroy me and my son together from the heritage of God.'
zh: 人要把我和我的兒子從 神的產業上一同消滅，也許王會垂聽，會把婢女從那人的手下救出來。

en: And your servant thought, 'The word of my lord the king will set me at rest,' for my lord the king is like the angel of God to discern good and evil. The LORD your God be with you!"
zh: 婢女又想：‘我主我王的話必能使我得安慰，因為我主我王像 神的使者一樣，能分辨是非。願耶和華你的 神與你同在。’”

en: Then the king answered the woman, "Do not hide from me anything I ask you." And the woman said, "Let my lord the king speak."
zh: 王對那婦人說：“我要問你一句話，請你不要瞞我。”婦人回答：“我主我王，請說！”

en: The king said, "Is the hand of Joab with you in all this?" The woman answered and said, "As surely as you live, my lord the king, one cannot turn to the right hand or to the left from anything that my lord the king has said. It was your servant Joab who commanded me; it was he who put all these words in the mouth of your servant.
zh: 王問：“這一切都是約押一手安排的嗎？”婦人回答：“我指著我主我王起誓，王所說的絲毫不差。這正是王的僕人約押吩咐我的，這一切話也都是他教婢女說的。

en: In order to change the course of things your servant Joab did this. But my lord has wisdom like the wisdom of the angel of God to know all things that are on the earth."
zh: 王的僕人約押這樣作，為要挽回這件事。我主有智慧，像 神使者的智慧一樣，能知道世上的一切事。”

en: Then the king said to Joab, "Behold now, I grant this; go, bring back the young man Absalom."
zh: 王對約押說：“看哪！這事我已經同意了。你去把那年輕人押沙龍帶回來吧！”

en: And Joab fell on his face to the ground and paid homage and blessed the king. And Joab said, "Today your servant knows that I have found favor in your sight, my lord the king, in that the king has granted the request of his servant."
zh: 約押就面伏於地，叩拜王，為王祝福；又說：“今天你僕人知道在我主我王眼前蒙了恩寵，因為王成全了你僕人所求的。”

en: So Joab arose and went to Geshur and brought Absalom to Jerusalem.
zh: 於是約押起來，往基述去，把押沙龍帶回耶路撒冷。

en: And the king said, "Let him dwell apart in his own house; he is not to come into my presence." So Absalom lived apart in his own house and did not come into the king's presence.
zh: 王說：“讓他回自己的家裡去，不要見王的面。”押沙龍就轉回自己的家裡去，沒有見王的面。

en: Now in all Israel there was no one so much to be praised for his handsome appearance as Absalom. From the sole of his foot to the crown of his head there was no blemish in him.
zh: 在全以色列中，沒有一個人像押沙龍那樣英俊，得人稱讚，從腳底到頭頂，都沒有一點缺陷。

en: And when he cut the hair of his head (for at the end of every year he used to cut it; when it was heavy on him, he cut it), he weighed the hair of his head, two hundred shekels by the king's weight.
zh: 他每年年底剪髮一次，因為他頭上的頭髮太厚，所以把它剪下來；他把頭髮剪下來以後，總是把頭髮稱一稱；按照王的法碼重約兩公斤。

en: There were born to Absalom three sons, and one daughter whose name was Tamar. She was a beautiful woman.
zh: 押沙龍生了三個兒子，一個女兒。女兒名叫他瑪，是個容貌美麗的女子。

en: So Absalom lived two full years in Jerusalem, without coming into the king's presence.
zh: 押沙龍在耶路撒冷住了兩年，都沒有見王的面。

en: Then Absalom sent for Joab, to send him to the king, but Joab would not come to him. And he sent a second time, but Joab would not come.
zh: 押沙龍派人去叫約押來，要託他去見王，約押卻不肯來見他。他第二次再派人去叫他，他還是不肯來。

en: Then he said to his servants, "See, Joab's field is next to mine, and he has barley there; go and set it on fire." So Absalom's servants set the field on fire.
zh: 於是押沙龍對僕人說：“你們看，約押的一塊田和我相鄰，田裡有大麥。你們去放火燒田！”押沙龍的僕人就放火把那塊田燒了。

en: Then Joab arose and went to Absalom at his house and said to him, "Why have your servants set my field on fire?"
zh: 於是約押起來，到了押沙龍的家，問他：“你的僕人為甚麼放火燒了我那塊田呢？”

en: Absalom answered Joab, "Behold, I sent word to you, 'Come here, that I may send you to the king, to ask, "Why have I come from Geshur? It would be better for me to be there still." Now therefore let me go into the presence of the king, and if there is guilt in me, let him put me to death.'"
zh: 押沙龍回答約押：“看哪，我派人去見你，說：‘請你到這裡來，我要託你去見王，替我說：“我為甚麼從基述回來呢？我若是還留在那邊，會覺得更好。”’現在，我要見王的面，如果我有罪，任由他把我殺死好了。”

en: Then Joab went to the king and told him, and he summoned Absalom. So he came to the king and bowed himself on his face to the ground before the king, and the king kissed Absalom.
zh: 於是約押進去見王，把這事告訴了他，王就召見押沙龍。押沙龍進去見王，在王面前臉伏在地拜他，王就與押沙龍親吻。
